[[🐛] Bug 'FirebaseAuth/FirebaseAuth-Swift.h' file not found (solution: you MUST use &#96;use_frameworks! :linkage =\u003e :static&#96; in Podfile) · Issue #8215 · invertase/react-native-firebase](https://github.com/invertase/react-native-firebase/issues/8215)\n3. [React Native iOS - \u0026#39;FirebaseAuth/FirebaseAuth-Swift.h\u0026#39; file not found (CocoaPods issue](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/79454334/react-native-ios-firebaseauth-firebaseauth-swift-h-file-not-found-cocoapods)\n4. [iOS build fails: 'FirebaseAuth/FirebaseAuth-Swift.h' file not found in React Native + FirebaseAuth setup · Issue #15249 · firebase/firebase-ios-sdk](https://github.com/firebase/firebase-ios-sdk/issues/15249)\n5. [FirebaseAuth/FirebaseAuth-Swift.h file not found in React Native iOS Project with Firebase Authentication](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/79637494/firebaseauth-firebaseauth-swift-h-file-not-found-in-react-native-ios-project-wit)\n\n# FirebaseAuth-Swift.h File Not Found Issue - Summary\n\nThis is a known issue in react-native-firebase when integrating Firebase Authentication with static frameworks on iOS.\n\n## Root Cause\nThe error occurs when Firebase pods are not properly configured as static frameworks, causing the Swift header file &#96;FirebaseAuth/FirebaseAuth-Swift.h&#96; to not be found during compilation.[1][2]\n\n## Primary Solution\n**Add &#96;use_frameworks! :linkage =\u003e :static&#96; to your Podfile:**[1][2]\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ruby\nuse_frameworks! :linkage =\u003e :static\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis forces CocoaPods to link Firebase as static frameworks, which is required for react-native-firebase compatibility.\n\n## Additional Configuration\nWhen using static frameworks, also add these pod specifications with modular headers:[2]\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ruby\n$RNFirebaseAsStaticFramework = true\npod 'GoogleUtilities', :modular_headers =\u003e true\npod 'Firebase', :modular_headers =\u003e true\npod 'FirebaseCore', :modular_headers =\u003e true\npod 'FirebaseAuth', :modular_headers =\u003e true\npod 'FirebaseCoreInternal', :modular_headers =\u003e true\npod 'FirebaseAuthInterop', :modular_headers =\u003e true\npod 'FirebaseCoreExtension', :modular_headers =\u003e true\npod 'RecaptchaInterop', :modular_headers =\u003e true\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Troubleshooting Steps\nIf the above doesn't resolve the issue, perform a clean reinstall:[1]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nrm -rf node_modules ios/Pods ios/Podfile.lock\nyarn install\ncd ios \u0026\u0026 pod install --repo-update\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Note:** Some users with the latest React Native versions report they may not need to manually add individual Firebase pod declarations.[3]",

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Identifying the issue: `FirebaseAuth/FirebaseAuth-Swift.h` is missing because Swift Firebase pods require `use_frameworks! :linkage => :static`. The Podfile only applies `use_frameworks!` when `USE_FRAMEWORKS` is set, so it was never enabled. Applying the official fix: defaulting to static frameworks and pinning the Firebase iOS SDK to match `@react-native-firebase/app` (11.8.0).

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Fixing header imports**

I'm investigating why `RCTPromiseRejectBlock` is undefined in RNFBFirestoreCommon.h, likely due to incorrect import order or framework settings. 

**Checking macro conflicts**

I'm investigating if `error` as a parameter name conflicts with macros in RNFBFirestoreCommon.h. I found that `error` as a parameter name likely conflicts with Apple's `errno.h` macro definitions. I found that `error` parameter name likely conflicts with Apple's `errno.h` macro definitions. I'm investigating whether the import order of Firebase and React headers causes macro conflicts that break React Native Firebase functionality. The RCT_EXPORT_METHOD macro is expanding incorrectly due to RCTBridgeModule not being parsed properly, likely related to static framework linking issues. 

**Fixing import paths**

I'm fixing the missing React-Core imports for RNFBFirestore static framework build. I'm checking if Firebase.h needs to be imported after React in the Podfile post_install hook. </details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Testing symlink behavior**

I'm investigating whether symlinks cause issues in the sandbox environment. 

**Investigating error macro**

I found that `error` might be a macro from Firebase or errno.h, which could break the RCTPromiseRejectBlock syntax. The `error` macro from Firebase/errno.h breaks `RCTPromiseRejectBlock` syntax by replacing the selector keyword `error`. Found that `error` macro from Firebase/errno.h is breaking `RCTPromiseRejectBlock` syntax by consuming the error parameter name. 

The `RCTPromiseRejectBlock` syntax error likely stems from Firebase's `error` macro conflicting with React Native's RCTPromiseRejectBlock type definition. 

**Checking firebase version**

I'm checking if upgrading @react-native-firebase packages to 22.x resolves the import order issue with FirebaseAuth-Swift.h. The React header resolution issue occurs with `use_frameworks! :linkage => :static` but can potentially be fixed with explicit dependencies in post_install. </details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Reordering imports**

I'm reordering imports to place React and Foundation before Firebase in the headers. I'm reordering imports to place React before Firebase, but RNFBFirestoreSerialize.h imports Firebase first, creating potential circular dependencies. I'm updating RNFBFirestoreCommon.h to import React and Firebase in correct order to avoid circular dependencies. I'm swapping React imports before Firebase in RNFBFirestoreCommon.h and RNFBFirestoreModule.h to prevent circular dependencies. I'm reordering imports in CollectionModule to group Firebase imports together and move React imports before RNFBFirestoreQuery.h. I'm adjusting CollectionModule's import order to place React imports before Firebase imports and ensure RNFBFirestoreQuery.h is correctly positioned. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Confirming `use_frameworks! :linkage => :static` fixes `FirebaseAuth-Swift.h`, but importing `<Firebase/Firebase.h>` before `<React/...>` breaks React typedefs (e.g. `RCTPromiseRejectBlock`) under static frameworks. Reordering imports in the Firestore package headers so React comes before Firebase.

+# Do not use global use_modular_headers! - it breaks Firestore gRPC-C++/gRPC-Core builds.
+$RNFirebaseAsStaticFramework = true
+# Match @react-native-firebase/app sdkVersions.ios.firebase — avoids mixed 11.8 / 11.15 resolution.
+$FirebaseSDKVersion = '11.8.0'
+
+# Swift Firebase pods (Auth, etc.) expose generated *-Swift.h only when pods are built as frameworks.
+# Default must be static frameworks; see https://rnfirebase.io/ and invertase/react-native-firebase#8215
 linkage = ENV['USE_FRAMEWORKS']
 if linkage != nil
   Pod::UI.puts "Configuring Pod with #{linkage}ally linked Frameworks".green
   use_frameworks! :linkage =&gt; linkage.to_sym
+else
+  use_frameworks! :linkage =&gt; :static
 end
 
 target 'Streamlist' do
   config = use_native_modules!
 
+  # Swift Firebase pods need module maps on these Obj-C deps when using static libs.
+  # Prefer this over global use_modular_headers! so gRPC-Core / gRPC-C++ still compile.
+  pod 'FirebaseCore', :modular_headers =&gt; true
+  pod 'FirebaseCoreExtension', :modular_headers =&gt; true
+  pod 'FirebaseCoreInternal', :modular_headers =&gt; true
+  pod 'FirebaseAuthInterop', :modular_headers =&gt; true
+  pod 'FirebaseAppCheckInterop', :modular_headers =&gt; true
+  pod 'FirebaseFirestoreInternal', :modular_headers =&gt; true
+  pod 'GoogleUtilities', :modular_headers =&gt; true
+  pod 'RecaptchaInterop', :modular_headers =&gt; true
+
   use_react_native!(
     :path =&gt; config[:reactNativePath],
     # An absolute path to your application root.
@@ -30,5 +51,12 @@ target 'Streamlist' do
       :mac_catalyst_enabled =&gt; false,
       # :ccache_enabled =&gt; true
     )
+    # gRPC / BoringSSL / abseil (via Firestore) + static Firebase: avoid
+    # -Werror=non-modular-include-in-framework-module and missing module map issues.
+    installer.pods_project.targets.each do |target|
+      target.build_configurations.each do |cfg|
+        cfg.build_settings['CLANG_ALLOW_NON_MODULAR_INCLUDES_IN_FRAMEWORK_MODULES'] = 'YES'
+      end
+    end
   end
 end

```
# Resolve react_native_pods.rb with node to allow for hoisting
require Pod::Executable.execute_command('node', ['-p',
  'require.resolve(
    "react-native/scripts/react_native_pods.rb",
    {paths: [process.argv[1]]},
  )', __dir__]).strip

platform :ios, min_ios_version_supported
prepare_react_native_project!

linkage = ENV['USE_FRAMEWORKS']
if linkage != nil
  Pod::UI.puts "Configuring Pod with #{linkage}ally linked Frameworks".green
  use_frameworks! :linkage =&gt; linkage.to_sym
end

target 'Streamlist' do
  config = use_native_modules!

  use_react_native!(
    :path =&gt; config[:reactNativePath],
    # An absolute path to your application root.
    :app_path =&gt; "#{Pod::Config.instance.installation_root}/.."
  )

  post_install do |installer|
    react_native_post_install(
      installer,
      config[:reactNativePath],
      :mac_catalyst_enabled =&gt; false,
      # :ccache_enabled =&gt; true
    )
  end
end

```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Analyzing launch screen**

I'm aligning the native window background with the surface color to fix the gap between splash and RN. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Identifying the cause: a gap between the native splash (dark) and the first React Navigation paint—default native/React roots are often white, and [`App.tsx`](phase2/streamlist/App.tsx) only sets `flex: 1` on the root with no `backgroundColor`.



_**Agent (composer-2)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "name": "Fix white screen before tabs",
  "overview": "The white flash happens in the brief window after the native splash is dismissed and before the JS tree and native-stack surfaces paint with your dark theme. Fix it by aligning root and navigator backgrounds with &#96;colors.surface&#96; and, on iOS, matching the window/root native background to the launch screen.",
  "plan": "# Fix white screen before tab navigator\n\n## What’s going wrong\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n  participant Native as Native_splash\n  participant Window as Window_RCT_root\n  participant JS as React_Navigation\n\n  Native-\u003e\u003eWindow: Splash dismissed\n  Note over Window: Default background often white\n  Window-\u003e\u003eJS: Bundle runs first frames\n  JS-\u003e\u003eWindow: Themed views paint\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- **[&#96;App.tsx&#96;](phase2/streamlist/App.tsx)** wraps the app in &#96;GestureHandlerRootView&#96; and &#96;SafeAreaProvider&#96; with &#96;style={{ flex: 1 }}&#96; only. There is **no &#96;backgroundColor&#96;**, so the first frames can show the **default native window/root color** (commonly white), especially on iOS.\n- **[&#96;RootNavigator.tsx&#96;](phase2/streamlist/src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx)** sets &#96;NavigationContainer&#96; &#96;theme.colors.background&#96; to &#96;colors.surface&#96;, but **native stack** and **bottom tabs** (via &#96;react-native-screens&#96;) can still use a **default light content background** for the native container until &#96;screenOptions&#96; explicitly set a dark &#96;contentStyle&#96; / card background.\n- **iOS**: [&#96;LaunchScreen.storyboard&#96;](phase2/streamlist/ios/Streamlist/LaunchScreen.storyboard) uses a dark view (~&#96;#131313&#96;, matching [&#96;colors.surface&#96;](phase2/streamlist/src/theme/colors.ts)). When the launch screen is replaced by the React root, **the &#96;UIWindow&#96; / root view is not guaranteed to stay that color** unless set in native code—hence a **white gap** between splash and tabs.\n- **Android**: [&#96;styles.xml&#96;](phase2/streamlist/android/app/src/main/res/values/styles.xml) already uses &#96;@drawable/splash_screen&#96; for &#96;windowBackground&#96;; any flash is often milder, but the same JS/native-stack fixes still help if you see it there.\n\n## Recommended fixes (in order)\n\n### 1. JS: paint the full root with the app surface color\n\nIn [&#96;App.tsx&#96;](phase2/streamlist/App.tsx), add &#96;backgroundColor: colors.surface&#96; to the root &#96;StyleSheet&#96; used by &#96;GestureHandlerRootView&#96; (and optionally the same on an outer &#96;View&#96; if you split layout). This is the **smallest, high-impact** change and matches project rules (colors from theme, no raw hex in random components—use &#96;colors.surface&#96;).\n\n### 2. JS: set native stack / tab screen backgrounds explicitly\n\nIn [&#96;RootNavigator.tsx&#96;](phase2/streamlist/src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx):\n\n- On **&#96;RootStack.Navigator&#96;**: add &#96;screenOptions&#96; with a dark background for the native stack container, e.g. &#96;contentStyle: { backgroundColor: colors.surface }&#96; (exact prop name follows your &#96;@react-navigation/native-stack&#96; version; if &#96;contentStyle&#96; is not supported on the root stack in your version, use the documented equivalent such as &#96;sceneContainerStyle&#96; / stack-specific option—verify against your installed stack types).\n- On **&#96;Tab.Navigator&#96;**: add the tab-level option that sets the **scene container** background to &#96;colors.surface&#96; (React Navigation’s docs: &#96;sceneContainerStyle&#96; or &#96;screenOptions&#96; field for background on the tab view—use what your types allow).\n\nThis prevents a **white native scaffold** behind the first tab while stacks mount.\n\n### 3. iOS native: match window background to splash / surface\n\nIn [&#96;AppDelegate.swift&#96;](phase2/streamlist/ios/Streamlist/AppDelegate.swift), after creating the &#96;UIWindow&#96; and/or after &#96;startReactNative(...)&#96;, set the window’s &#96;backgroundColor&#96; to the same dark gray as the launch screen (e.g. &#96;UIColor(red: 19/255, green: 19/255, blue: 19/255, alpha: 1)&#96; for &#96;#131313&#96;). Optionally set the same on &#96;window?.rootViewController?.view?.backgroundColor&#96; if a flash persists (some RN versions need both).\n\nKeep **one comment** in Swift that the RGB matches &#96;colors.surface&#96; / LaunchScreen so future theme changes stay intentional.\n\n### 4. Verify (manual)\n\n- Cold launch on **iOS Simulator and device**: splash → **no white frame** → tabs.\n- Repeat on **Android** if needed.\n- Run &#96;npm test&#96; and &#96;npm run lint&#96; from [&#96;phase2/streamlist/&#96;](phase2/streamlist/) per repo practice.\n\n## Out of scope unless still flashing\n\n- **Splash libraries** (e.g. keeping splash visible until JS “ready”)—only if native + JS background fixes are insufficient.\n- **Deferring first render** until data loads—your navigator does not gate on &#96;watchlistStore.hydrated&#96; for showing tabs, so hydration is unlikely the main cause of a **pre-tab** white screen.\n",
  "todos": [
    {
      "content": "Add colors.surface background to root in App.tsx (GestureHandlerRootView / SafeAreaProvider as needed)",
      "id": "root-bg",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Set dark background on RootStack and Tab navigators in RootNavigator.tsx (contentStyle / sceneContainerStyle per API)",
      "id": "nav-bg",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Set UIWindow (and optionally root VC view) backgroundColor in AppDelegate.swift to match #131313",
      "id": "ios-window",
      "status": "pending"
    },
    {
      "content": "Cold-launch iOS/Android; run npm test \u0026\u0026 npm run lint",
      "id": "verify",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Here’s what was implemented.

### New: [`MainTabHeader.tsx`](phase2/streamlist/src/components/common/MainTabHeader.tsx)
- Same layout as Home: **movie icon** + **“StreamList”** on the left, **notifications** on the right (same typography and spacing as before).

### Wired into all main tab roots
- **Home** – Replaced the inline header with `<MainTabHeader />` and removed duplicate header styles from `HomeScreen.tsx`.
- **Search** – Removed the old bar (text + profile avatar); search field stays directly under the shared header.
- **Watchlist** – Uses the shared header for loading, empty state, and the filled list; **“YOUR COLLECTION” / “My Watchlist”** sit in a `titleBlock` below (the old profile icon on the right is removed so it matches Home).
- **Profile** – Shared header at the top; the separate **“Profile”** title was removed so the top matches the other tabs (content still starts with the avatar block).

Notifications still go to **`ProfileTab` → `Notifications`** everywhere, same as Home.

Tests and ESLint both pass.

---
